markdownRemark.excerpt: For nearly two weeks I have been ruminating over writing about running. The most simple sport in the world, the one that changed my life and my relationship…
markdownRemark.html:
For nearly two weeks I have been ruminating over writing about running. The most simple sport in the world, the one that changed my life and my relationship with my body. I know some people don’t feel runners high but I have never once finished a 16 mile run and not felt better than I did when I started. There is something about pounding the pavement, the most repetivive motion in the world, taking one of four routes available to me each day, that as much as it should seem like it should never does actaully get old.

But this love letter isn’t going to be what I planned. Four days ago I was diagnosed with a tibia stress fracture in my right leg. I get an MRI on Tuesday to confirm but based on the localized pain, swelling, and ability to walk, all signs point to a 6-8 week period without running.

Instead, this love letter will be one dedicated to moving forward.


There are many reasons variation and changes occur, especially over a longer period of time and when a system scales. It’s difficult to scope out a project and forsee all the ways in which it will interact in the larger system by the time it’s implemented.

Onward.

frontmatter.title: On Running
frontmatter.description: Notes on my favourite thing in the world to be doing and having it taken away.

frontmatter.title: On Not Being Afraid of Tech Debt
